The provided excerpt contains no specific entities, dates, definitions, procedures, claims, tables, figures, metrics, or evidence types, and therefore cannot answer detailed factual, procedural, or data-driven queries without additional content."

    "summary": "This section provides a structured evaluation of a long-running turn-based card RPG, designed to answer retrieval queries across the following dimensions:\n- **Topics:** Game review covering audio-visual presentation, core combat, content/systems, operations/monetization/community, and device compatibility.\n- **Entities & Features:** Shikigami (式神), Soul system (御魂), UR/SSR rarity tiers, PVE Secret Realms (秘闻副本), PVP Arena (斗技), LBS social features, official desktop emulator, DianDian (2025).\n- **Dates & Timeline:** 2025 (UR shikigami introduction, copyright year); approximately 9 years of continuous operation.\n- **Definitions & Procedures:** Turn-based strategy combat centered on skill timing, shikigami combos, and soul set matching; gacha monetization with a free UR acquisition path for players who collect all SSRs; iterative UI/modeling updates; official \"developer letters\" for community feedback.\n- **Claims & Evaluations:** Japanese-style art and music maintain high quality with improved modeling over time; combat offers deep strategy but PVP meta shifts rapidly with new units; soul stat RNG is a persistent player complaint; update/event rhythm is stable; community and fan creation are highly active; device compatibility is well-optimized with near-PC experience via emulator.\n- **Metrics & Scoring:** Evaluation weights: Core Combat/Gameplay (30%), Audio-Visual (25%), Content/Systems (20%), Operations/Community (15%), Performance/Compatibility (10%). Category scores: 84, 83, 82, 84, 90 (out of 100). Overall composite score: 84.9.\n- **Evidence Types:** Reviewer analysis, documented player feedback (praise for audio-visuals, criticism of soul RNG), operational claims (update frequency, longevity), and feature specifications."
  }
]

message.content: This parent section presents a structured review and analysis of the game "Onmyoji" (《阴阳师》) by DianDian Data (点点数据), organized across five subsections. While the first four subsections ("Visual and Audio Performance," "Core Combat and Gameplay Experience," "Game Content and Systems," and "Operations, Commercialization, and Community") function exclusively as structural headings without substantive content, the final subsection ("Performance and Device Compatibility") consolidates the complete evaluation. The review covers topics including audio-visual presentation, turn-based combat mechanics, game systems, monetization, community engagement, and device optimization. Key entities and features include Shikigami (式神), the Soul system (御魂), UR/SSR rarity tiers, PVE Secret Realms, PVP Arena, LBS social features, and an official desktop emulator. Dates and timeline references include 2025 (noting the UR shikigami introduction and copyright year) and approximately nine years of continuous operation. Definitions and procedures detail turn-based strategy combat focused on skill timing, shikigami combos, and soul set matching; a gacha monetization model featuring a free UR acquisition path for players who collect all SSRs; iterative UI/modeling updates; and official "developer letters" for community feedback. Claims and evaluations highlight sustained high-quality Japanese-style art and music, deep strategic combat offset by rapid PVP meta shifts, persistent player complaints regarding soul stat RNG, stable update rhythms, highly active community and fan creation, and well-optimized device compatibility offering a near-PC experience via emulator. No tables or figures are visible in the provided excerpts. Metrics specify evaluation weights (Core Combat 30%, Audio-Visual 25%, Content/Systems 20%, Operations/Community 15%, Performance 10%) and corresponding category scores (84, 83, 82, 84, 90 out of 100), yielding an overall composite score of 84.9. Evidence types consist of reviewer analysis, documented player feedback, operational claims, and feature specifications.
